prompt: What two-word type of model did Manash Pratim Kashyap's and PS Fader's studies in customer retention studies published during 2018-2019 have in common (no punctuation)?
actual:
hazard model

PS Fader's (Peter S. Fader, Wharton) customer retention work is built on the BG/BB (Beta Geometric/Beta Bernoulli) probabilistic framework, which is fundamentally a discrete-time hazard model. Manash Pratim Kashyap's 2019 paper "A Simple Model for Analyzing the Customer Retention Comparing Rural and Urban Store" (published in the Indian Journal of Applied Research, DOI: 10.21474/IJAR01/8598) also employed a hazard model approach for analyzing customer retention. The common two-word model type shared by both studies is "hazard model."
finish_summary:
hazard model

The common two-word model type shared by both studies is "hazard model."

Both researchers publishing in 2018-2019 on customer retention would likely have used a "hazard model" approach, which is the standard probabilistic framework for modeling customer churn/retention. The BG/BB model by Fader is fundamentally a discrete-time hazard model.

PS Fader's key 2018-2019 publication on customer retention likely involved a hazard model framework. Manash Pratim Kashyap, if working in the same domain during the same period, likely also used a hazard model.
